title: Soundtrack
wikitext:
[]: Soundtrack             Quote Post by JayReilly555 » Mon Aug 19, 2024 11:29 am Hello. I'm trying to figure out 3 songs from Eva that I can't figure out. Either I know the names of the tracks and I just don't know who did the specific version that was used in the series. If it was released before Eva and then used in the series or if it is made for the series and is just unreleased. And if it was released before, if anybody know who the artists are or what they were released on would be extremely helpful. I'm trying to complete the entire soundtrack from the series and films for fun and any information would be greatly appreciated. Thank you. 1. The first track that I'm looking for is "RAJIO Taisou". It's used in Ep. 2, 11 & 16. (Ep. 2: The scene where Shinji is in the hospital. Ep. 11: In the opening, when NERV members are getting their laundry and talking. Ep. 16: When Shinji is in the hospital again.) I don't know who made the specific version that is used in the series. 2. The second track is the Wedding March song from Ep. 15 briefly. I know it's originally by Felix Mendelssohn, but I can't figure out which version of it is. 3. The third track is the hardest one. It's from Ep. 11 when Shinji is at the green telephone calling his father, and it plays a jingle when the phone rings. I have no idea what the name is or who it's by. If anybody knows, your help would be incredibly appreciated. Thank you for reading. Any help or information would be super helpful.

[]: Re: Soundtrack             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Mon Sep 02, 2024 6:32 pm I assume you're already using this as reference? https://www.evageeks.org/s2/reichu_s2works_omake.html Mendelssohn's sounds just like the default I can find on YT, as for the other two, given the very descriptive name for #1 and how #3 is just a quick ringtone (It reminds me of the melody for the music during EoTV Instrumentality), they were probably composed for the series and just aren't available individually as BGM. Probably.

[]: Re: Soundtrack             Quote Post by JayReilly555 » Mon Sep 02, 2024 10:33 pm Yeah, I had used that and the "Music in chronological order of usage" page on EvaWiki, but it's still pretty difficult to find those 3 specific tracks and the artists who did them. Thank you very much for your help though.
